purpose: Identifies the critical effective tariff rate at which pass-through behavior shifts from primarily firm-absorbed to primarily consumer-borne, with implications for both inflation and credit
components: Below ~15% effective rate: 30-50% pass-through, firms absorb via margin compression, coverage ratios manageable
15-25% effective rate: transition zone, pass-through rising, coverage ratios deteriorating
Above ~25% effective rate: 60-90% pass-through, margin compression exhausted, fallen angel risk materializes
current_assessment: Current effective tariff regime (~12-18% weighted average) sits at the cusp of the danger zone. The difference between 15% and 30% effective tariffs is not 2x the inflation impact but 3-4x due to nonlinearity. Independent derivation from credit (coverage ratio arithmetic) and macro (empirical pass-through literature) frameworks strengthens confidence.
limitations: Threshold likely varies by sector, firm size, and competitive structure
Calibrated primarily on 2018-2019 tariff data — may not hold for broader product coverage
Does not account for currency adjustment offsetting tariff impact
Lag structure between tariff imposition and threshold crossing not specified
